engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking a physical restaurant — dish/interior photos, menu with prices, opening hours and address ARE the decision. Not selling defined expertise (not trust) and no portfolio-as-proof (not work).

goal.one_action: Book a table (reservation); secondary: view menu / join mailing list

structure.archetype_note: Restaurant brochure: home (story + gallery), menu page with prices + PDF menu links, ResDiary reservation page, contact/hours. Newsletter capture repeated across pages.

mapped.observed_as: Contemporary Italian Cooking in Belfast City Centre

unmapped.observed: Menu page links to 6 downloadable PDF menus (Low Gluten, Full Menu Spring 2026, Kids, Drinks, Plant Based, Set)
unmapped.why_no_fit: A cluster of downloadable document variants of the menu — no canonical section for a PDF document set; not features/faq

unmapped.observed: Top promo banner: 'Graduation Menu Now Available! Click here'
unmapped.why_no_fit: Sitewide announcement/promo bar, no canonical section id

proposed_new.name: external-booking-redirect
proposed_new.evidence: Primary BOOK NOW CTA loads a third-party ResDiary reservation widget (hidden input name='rdwidgeturl'); scheduling inventory/conversion happens in the embedded off-site engine, not native to the site.

confidence.why: fetch_quality=rendered (capped at medium); book-now form fields only materialized after render via ResDiary embed, so booking flow inferred from rendered widget rather than clean static capture.

gap_findings: No canonical capability/component for a restaurant MENU with priced sections — mapped to pricing but it is a menu-list, not a pricing/packages table. A 'menu' component/capability is missing from the closed vocab.
Downloadable PDF menu variants (dietary/kids/drinks/set) have no home in sections or capabilities — a document/PDF-set pattern recurs for restaurants.
Sitewide promo/announcement banner has no canonical section.
